title: ASCII Fields, Split Panes, Ripple Type & Histogram Sliders
description: Five fresh UI drops: a mouse-reactive ASCII particle field, a resizable 3-pane workspace, a canvas water-ripple text distortion, ASCII/pixelate/glitch hover dissolves, and dual-handle histogram range sliders.

drops.title: ASCII Blob
drops.description: A full-screen field of monospace glyphs ripples like liquid away from the cursor and bursts outward on click.
drops.concept: A grid of ASCII characters covers the viewport; each glyph's brightness comes from a layered sine-based value-noise field animated over time. Cursor proximity adds a decaying radial ripple that displaces and brightens nearby glyphs, and a click triggers an expanding shockwave ring that scatters characters before they settle back.

interaction.mouse: Move to ripple the field; click for a burst shockwave
interaction.click: Adjust density, ripple strength and speed in the top-right panel (desktop only)
interaction.touch: Drag a finger to ripple; tap to burst

drops.title: Split-Pane Workspace
drops.description: A 3-pane editor-style layout with draggable dividers, double-click-to-reset, and widths that persist across reloads.
drops.concept: Resizable multi-pane workspaces (popularized by libraries like split.js and react-resizable-panels) are the backbone of modern web IDEs and dashboards. This drop implements the core mechanic from scratch: pointer-driven divider dragging that adjusts flex-basis with clamped min/max widths, double-click resets per divider, and layout persistence to localStorage.
drops.source: Inspired by split.js (nathancahill/split) and react-resizable-panels patterns

interaction.mouse: Drag either vertical divider; double-click a divider to reset that side
interaction.click: Reset Layout button restores both panes to defaults
interaction.touch: Drag dividers with a finger the same way

drops.title: Ripple Text
drops.description: Drag across a headline and it distorts like water — a canvas offscreen-buffer slice displacement, no WebGL.
drops.concept: The text is rendered once to an offscreen canvas buffer. On each frame, the visible canvas is redrawn in thin horizontal strips, each strip's horizontal offset computed as the sum of decaying sine waves centered on recent pointer positions (distance-from-ripple-origin drives phase and amplitude, both decaying with age). This reproduces the classic Blotter.js/GSAP liquid-text look using only 2D canvas slice-blitting.
drops.source: Inspired by comrx — "Liquid distortion text hover effect (blotter.js and gsap)" (CodePen)

interaction.mouse: Drag across the word to ripple it; amplitude/decay sliders tune the wave
interaction.click: Click once for a single ripple pulse
interaction.touch: Drag a finger across the text

drops.title: Hover Dissolve
drops.description: Hovering a scene dissolves it live into ASCII glyphs, chunky pixel blocks, or a scanline glitch — three swappable pixel-buffer effects.
drops.concept: Each card renders a procedurally generated gradient scene once. On hover, a per-frame effect pass reads the scene's pixel buffer via getImageData: ASCII mode maps per-cell luminance to a character ramp and draws it with fillText in the source color; pixelate mode downsamples to a tiny canvas then upscales with image smoothing disabled; glitch mode slices the image into jittered horizontal bands with an additive color-channel offset.

interaction.mouse: Hover any card to dissolve it into its effect; move away to restore
interaction.click: Tap/click a card to toggle the effect on and off
interaction.touch: Tap a card to toggle its dissolve effect

drops.title: Histogram Range Sliders
drops.description: Dual-handle range sliders with a live distribution histogram behind the track and value tooltips that follow each handle.
drops.concept: Dual-handle range inputs (as popularized by noUiSlider) are reimplemented from scratch: two independently draggable circular handles constrained to never cross, a filled range bar between them, a synthetic bell-shaped histogram rendered as background bars that highlight when inside the selected range, live tooltips above each handle, and full keyboard arrow-key support with proper ARIA slider roles.

interaction.mouse: Drag either handle; hover shows the live value tooltip
interaction.click: Click and drag from either handle
